title: Add bin/podman dependency to install.bin
body:
Also make the bin targets phony and redirect

Normally a make target should create itself

Closes #2702

comments.body:
Hi @afbjorklund. Thanks for your PR.

I'm waiting for a [containers](https://github.com/orgs/containers/people) or [openshift](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) member to verify that this patch is reasonable to test. If it is, they should reply with `/ok-to-test` on its own line. Until that is done, I will not automatically test new commits in this PR, but the usual testing commands by org members will still work. Regular contributors should [join the org](https://github.com/orgs/openshift/people) to skip this step.

Once the patch is verified, the new status will be reflected by the `ok-to-test` label.

I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod).

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository.
</details>

Can one of the admins verify this patch?
I understand the following commands:
- `bot, add author to whitelist`
- `bot, test pull request`
- `bot, test pull request once`

Even though the Makefile is a little dirty, currently there is a large PHONY section down below, could you move your new PHONYs down there, as well as the couple that got added randomly.

Thanks

[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *afbjorklund*, *mheon* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Flibpod). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Changes LGTM, but images test is still unhappy.

@afbjorklund: The following test **failed**, say `/retest` to rerun them all:

Test name | Commit | Details | Rerun command
--- | --- | --- | ---
ci/prow/images | 6c1595a0e2a5d16be92f4ed10c33af012b97755d | [link](https://openshift-gce-devel.appspot.com/build/origin-ci-test/pr-logs/pull/containers_libpod/2923/pull-ci-containers-libpod-master-images/4703) | `/test images`

[Full PR test history](https://openshift-gce-devel.appspot.com/pr/containers_libpod/2923). [Your PR dashboard](https://openshift-gce-devel.appspot.com/pr/afbjorklund). Please help us cut down on flakes by [linking to](https://github.com/kubernetes/community/blob/master/contributors/devel/flaky-tests.md#filing-issues-for-flaky-tests) an [open issue](https://github.com/containers/libpod/issues?q=is:issue+is:open) when you hit one in your PR.

<details>

Instructions for interacting with me using PR comments are available [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/pull-requests.md).  If you have questions or suggestions related to my behavior, please file an issue against the [kubernetes/test-infra](https://github.com/kubernetes/test-infra/issues/new?title=Prow%20issue:) repository. I understand the commands that are listed [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands).
</details>
<!-- test report -->
@mheon Any idea what is going on with the test system?

I think this might be a legitimate failure out of the rpm build task in images
@baude PTAL

It is a legit failure and I pulled the PR and it is reproducible.

From the build log ....
```go build -ldflags ' -X github.com/containers/libpod/libpod.gitCommit=b3d2266fccab2e308d52c4df28b8157753dea6d8 -X github.com/containers/libpod/libpod.buildInfo=1555589214' -tags "seccomp   ostree selinux  varlink exclude_graphdriver_devicemapper" -o bin/podman github.com/containers/libpod/cmd/podman
# github.com/containers/libpod/cmd/podman/shared
../../../cmd/podman/shared/intermediate_varlink.go:72:42: undefined: iopodman.Create
../../../cmd/podman/shared/intermediate_varlink.go:73:7: undefined: iopodman.Create
../../../cmd/podman/shared/intermediate_varlink.go:317:34: undefined: iopodman.Create
```
This tells us that the RPM spec uses go build (not make) and that the equiv of make varlink_generate is not being called prior.  Because varlink is in the build tags (as it should be), the build fails.
Cannot be fixed, due to rebuild issues with the rpm spec file.
